description: Bar inventory loss detection and profitability platform for bar owners

features.name: Login
features.description: Sign in to your BarGuard account
how_to_use: Enter your username (or email)
Enter your password
Click Sign In — OR click Sign in with Google for OAuth
features.notes: Usernames are internally mapped to username@barguard.app. Google OAuth is supported as an alternative.

features.name: Sign Up
features.description: Create a new BarGuard bar owner account
how_to_use: Enter your bar name
Choose a username (letters, numbers, underscore, hyphen only)
Enter your email (for recovery)
Set a password (6+ characters)
Click Create Account — OR use Sign up with Google
features.notes: After signup you are routed to a check-email page. Email must be verified before full access.

features.name: Forgot Password
features.description: Request a password reset link via email
how_to_use: Click 'Forgot password' on the login page
Enter your username
Click Send Reset Link
Check your email for the link (expires in 1 hour)
Click the link and set a new password

features.name: Email Verification
features.description: Verify your email address after signup
how_to_use: After signup, check your email for a verification link
Click the link — system confirms your address automatically
If expired, return to the check-email page and click Resend

features.name: Welcome / Onboarding
features.description: First-time setup flow guiding new users from account creation to profile and plan selection
how_to_use: After email verification, you land on the Welcome page
Read the 14-day free trial notice
Click 'Set Up My Bar Profile' to continue to profile setup
After profile setup you are directed to choose a pricing plan
features.notes: Three-step progress shown: Account → Profile → Plan

features.name: Partner Login
features.description: Separate login portal for partner/vendor accounts (not bar owners)
how_to_use: Navigate to /partner-login
Enter partner email and password
System validates that the account has a 'partner' role
Redirect to partner dashboard on success
features.notes: Partner accounts are separate from bar owner accounts and only have access to their partner dashboard.

features.name: Bar Information
features.description: Edit your bar's business details
how_to_use: Go to Profile (sidebar or menu)
Edit: bar name, address, phone number, bar type, contact email
Click Save Changes
features.notes: Contact email is used for reorder emails, reports, and account recovery.

features.name: Change Username
features.description: Update your login username
how_to_use: Go to Profile
Enter new username in the username field
Click Update Username
features.notes: Must be alphanumeric plus underscore or hyphen.

features.name: Change Password
features.description: Update your account password
how_to_use: Go to Profile
Enter new password (6+ characters)
Confirm the new password
Click Update Password

features.name: Billing & Plan Management
features.description: View your current plan and upgrade or manage your subscription
how_to_use: Go to Profile → scroll to Billing section
Your current plan is displayed
To upgrade: click Upgrade next to Pro or Enterprise
You are redirected to Stripe checkout to complete payment
To manage or cancel: click 'Manage subscription' — redirects to Stripe billing portal

plans.name: Pro
plans.description: Everything in Basic + reorder management & POS integrations

plans.name: Enterprise
plans.description: Everything in Pro + up to 5 locations

sections.name: Dashboard & Variance Monitoring
sections.description: The core loss detection engine — run shift or date-range calculations to detect inventory shrinkage

features.name: Shift-Based Variance Calculation
features.description: Calculate how much inventory was used vs how much should have been used based on sales — surfacing over-pours, spillage, and theft
how_to_use: Select a calculation mode:
OPTION A — Preset Shift: Choose a shift (Morning, Lunch, Happy Hour, Dinner, Late Night, Full Day), pick a service date, click Run Calculations
OPTION B — Live Shift: Toggle 'Use Current Time' to analyze an ongoing shift in real-time
OPTION C — Custom Date Range: Set From date, To date, optional start/end times, click Run Calculations
Results appear as KPI cards, a risk table, and an AI summary option
shift_presets: Late Night (spans midnight — ends next day)
features.notes: Late Night shift handles overnight spanning automatically. Custom times require both start and end to be set.

features.name: AI Summary
features.description: Generate an AI-written natural-language summary of the variance results
how_to_use: Run a variance calculation first
Click the 'AI Summary' button in the results area
Wait a moment — the system analyzes the variance data
Read the markdown-formatted breakdown including critical items, warnings, trends, and revenue context
features.notes: Includes revenue and covers-per-shift context when POS data is available.

features.name: Variance KPI Cards
features.description: At-a-glance summary metrics from the last calculation

metrics.name: Critical Items
metrics.description: Count of items with severe variance — immediate attention required

metrics.name: Warning Items
metrics.description: Count of items with moderate variance — monitor closely

metrics.name: Healthy Items
metrics.description: Count of items within acceptable range

metrics.name: Est. Loss
metrics.description: Dollar estimate of loss based on item cost prices + variance oz

metrics.name: Health Score
metrics.description: 0–100 score. Red < 40, Amber < 70, Green >= 70. Shows highest-risk item name on hover.

features.name: Risk Table
features.description: Itemized variance results for every inventory item tracked in the shift

features.name: Next Actions Panel
features.description: Recommended actions for items that need attention, based on status
how_to_use: Panel appears in results after calculation
Review recommended actions for critical and warning items
Actions may include: check pour counts, review recipe, inspect storage

features.name: Performance Summary
features.description: Business performance metrics for the calculated period (requires POS connection)

features.name: Reorder Alerts Widget
features.description: Shows items currently below their minimum stock threshold directly on the dashboard
how_to_use: Set reorder levels on inventory items
Widget updates automatically when counts fall below the threshold
Click through to the full Reorder page for details

features.name: Viewing Context Banner
features.description: Displays what period/shift you are currently viewing — e.g., 'Dinner · Mon Mar 9 · 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM'
features.notes: Shows a pulsing live indicator if you are viewing a currently-running shift.

features.name: Clear Results
features.description: Reset all displayed calculation data
how_to_use: Click the trash icon in the results area
Confirm the dialog
All KPIs, risk table, AI summary, and performance data are cleared

features.name: Inventory Items (Master List)
features.description: Manage the master list of every bottle, keg, food item, or supply you track
how_to_use: Go to Inventory Items in the sidebar
View all items in a searchable, filterable table
To add: click New Item, fill in name/category/unit/cost, save
To edit: click an item row, update fields, save
To delete: click delete icon on the item row
item_fields: Cost per unit (used in loss dollar calculations)

sub_features.name: CSV Bulk Import
sub_features.description: Upload a CSV with item names, units, and costs to create many items at once
sub_features.how_to_use: Click Import → upload CSV file with columns: name, unit, cost_per_unit

sub_features.name: Bulk Category Update
sub_features.description: Select multiple items and assign them to a category in one action
sub_features.how_to_use: Check items → click Bulk Update Categories → select/type category → Apply

sub_features.name: Bulk Price Update
sub_features.description: Update cost_per_unit for multiple items simultaneously
sub_features.how_to_use: Check items → click Bulk Update Prices → enter new cost → Apply

sub_features.name: AI Auto-Categorize
sub_features.description: Let Claude AI read your item names and assign categories automatically
sub_features.how_to_use: Click AI Categorize → review suggested categories → confirm or adjust → save

features.name: Stock Levels / Inventory Counts
features.description: Record physical on-hand quantities for each inventory item
how_to_use: Go to Stock in the sidebar
View current quantity on hand for each item
Update counts manually by entering a number in the count field
Or scan barcodes using the barcode scan feature
Or bulk import counts via CSV
Counts are timestamped — each count record has a counted_at date

sub_features.name: Barcode Scanning
sub_features.description: Use a device camera or barcode scanner to scan bottle barcodes and auto-increment counts
sub_features.how_to_use: Click Scan Bottles → point camera at barcode → item is identified and count is updated

sub_features.name: CSV Count Import
sub_features.description: Upload a CSV of your physical count (item, qty) instead of entering manually
sub_features.how_to_use: Go to Uploads → Inventory Count tab → upload CSV

features.name: Menu Items
features.description: Define every item you sell — drinks and food — so sales data can be matched to recipes
how_to_use: Go to Menu Items in the sidebar
View all menu items in a categorized list
Click New Item: enter name, category, item type (beverage/food)
Edit: click a row → update → save
Delete: click delete icon

sub_features.name: Bulk Import
sub_features.description: Import many menu items at once from a CSV or from your POS catalog
sub_features.how_to_use: Click Import → upload CSV — OR — use POS connection to import catalog

features.name: Recipe Management
features.description: Define what inventory items and quantities go into each menu item — this is the basis for expected usage calculations
how_to_use: From a menu item, click Edit Recipes
Add ingredient rows: select inventory item, enter quantity (oz), optional notes
Save the recipe
Recipes are used by the variance engine to calculate expected usage from sales data

sub_features.name: AI Recipe Generate
sub_features.description: Describe a drink and Claude AI will generate an ingredient list with standard quantities
sub_features.how_to_use: On the recipe form, click AI Generate → type drink name or description → review and confirm ingredients

sub_features.name: AI Recipe Match
sub_features.description: Upload a list of drink names and AI will automatically match them to your existing inventory items
sub_features.how_to_use: Go to Recipes → AI Match → paste or upload drink names → review matches → confirm

sub_features.name: Recipe Suggestions
sub_features.description: System suggests ingredient combinations based on drink name patterns
sub_features.how_to_use: Suggestions appear automatically when you type a menu item name — click to accept

features.name: Sales Log
features.description: View detailed itemized sales history — what was sold, when, how much revenue, matched or unmatched to menu items
how_to_use: Go to Sales in the sidebar
Choose view mode:
SINGLE NIGHT: pick a date with the date picker, use Prev/Next to navigate days, or click Today
DATE RANGE: switch to range mode, set From and To dates, click View
Filter by type: All / Drinks / Food
Filter by station: All Stations / specific station / Unassigned
View summary KPIs: Revenue, Items Sold, Unique Items
View itemized table with qty, unit price, gross sales, and match status per item
columns: Matched status (linked to menu or unlinked)
features.notes: Revenue is only shown if price data is available from POS. Unmatched items show a badge indicating they have no recipe linked.

features.name: POS Modifier Rules
features.description: Define how POS modifiers (like 'Extra Shot', 'No Bacon', 'Double') affect recipe ingredient quantities
how_to_use: Go to Modifier Rules in the sidebar
View list of modifiers seen in your POS sales history
For each modifier, choose an action:
IGNORE — no change to recipe
REMOVE ingredient — subtract an inventory item (enter qty + unit)
ADD ingredient — add an inventory item (enter qty + unit)
MULTIPLY recipe — scale entire recipe by a factor (e.g., 2x for a double)
Click Save for each row
Rules apply automatically during variance calculations
features.notes: Modifiers are auto-populated from your POS history. Rules persist and apply going forward.

features.name: Drink Library
features.description: A reference library of drink recipes — separate from your menu items — useful for standardizing recipes across staff
how_to_use: Go to Drink Library in the sidebar
Search drinks by name or filter by category
Click a drink to see full recipe: ingredients, instructions, glassware, garnish
To add a new drink:
Click Add Drink
Enter name, category, glassware, garnish, instructions, notes
Add ingredient rows (ingredient name, qty in oz, optional notes)
Click Save
To populate a starter library: click 'Import Popular Drinks'

sub_features.name: Import Popular Drinks
sub_features.description: Seed your library with a set of standard popular cocktail recipes in one click
sub_features.how_to_use: Click Import Popular Drinks button — confirms, then populates the library

features.name: Sales CSV Upload
features.description: Import a POS sales export CSV into BarGuard
how_to_use: Go to Uploads → Sales tab
Upload your CSV file (columns: date, item name, quantity, price — optional)
System parses rows and imports them
If any item names are unrecognized, an alias resolver appears
For each unmatched name: select the matching menu item from the dropdown or click Create to add a new one
Confirm — import is complete
features.notes: Alias mappings are saved permanently — the same raw name will auto-match next time.

features.name: Inventory Count CSV Upload
features.description: Import physical inventory counts from a spreadsheet
how_to_use: Go to Uploads → Inventory Count tab
Upload CSV with columns: count date, item name, qty on hand
System imports counts with timestamps
Unmatched item names go through alias resolution

features.name: Purchases CSV Upload
features.description: Import historical purchase/receiving logs from a spreadsheet
how_to_use: Go to Uploads → Purchases tab
Upload CSV with columns: purchase date, item name, qty purchased (+ optional unit cost)
System creates purchase records for each row

features.name: Invoice / Receipt Scanning
features.description: Photograph or upload an invoice PDF and AI extracts all line items automatically
how_to_use: Go to Purchase Scan Import in the sidebar
Click Upload New Receipt
Select an image or PDF of an invoice
System runs OCR and AI extraction — creates a draft import
Go to the draft review page
Review extracted line items: item name, qty, unit, confidence score
Toggle 'View Raw Text' to see the raw OCR output if needed
Edit any incorrectly extracted fields
Match each line to an existing inventory item — or click Create to add a new one
Click Confirm Import — inventory purchase records are saved
features.notes: Drafts are saved and can be revisited before confirming. Confidence scores show how certain the AI is about each extracted line.

features.name: Purchase Import Draft Management
features.description: View and manage all uploaded invoice drafts awaiting review
how_to_use: Go to Purchase Scan Import — the list view shows all drafts
Drafts are grouped by upload month
Click a draft to open the review page
Delete a draft if it was uploaded in error

features.name: Item Name Alias Resolution
features.description: Map raw POS or invoice names to your normalized inventory and menu items — solves the problem of 'Bud Lt' vs 'Budweiser Light'
how_to_use: Go to Alias Resolution in the sidebar
View two sections: unmatched Menu Names and unmatched Inventory Names
For each unmatched raw name, select the correct item from the dropdown
Click Save for each row
Progress shows 'X resolved, Y pending'
Once all resolved, you see a success state
features.notes: Aliases are permanent — once saved, the same raw name will auto-match on every future upload. Alias resolution can also be triggered inline after a CSV upload.

features.name: Inline Sales Alias Resolver
features.description: Resolve unmatched sales item names directly after a CSV upload without leaving the upload flow
how_to_use: Upload a sales CSV
If unresolved names exist, the resolver appears on the same page
Match names or click Create to create a new menu item
Click Skip to leave unmatched items for later

features.name: POS Connections Manager
features.description: Connect BarGuard to your POS system to automatically sync sales data
how_to_use: Go to Connections in the sidebar
View all supported POS providers and their connection status
Click Connect for your provider
For OAuth providers (Square, Clover, Lightspeed): you are redirected to the provider's auth screen — grant access — redirected back as connected
For credential providers (Toast, Heartland, Focus): enter your API key or username/password in the form
Once connected, click Sync to pull the latest sales data
View sync logs showing last sync time and status

sub_features.name: Import POS Catalog
sub_features.description: Pull your menu items directly from your POS into BarGuard — saves manual entry
sub_features.how_to_use: After connecting a provider, click 'Import Items' — a modal shows your POS catalog with checkboxes — select items, set units, click Import

sub_features.name: Sync Logs
sub_features.description: View timestamped history of all sync jobs with status
sub_features.how_to_use: On the Connections page, scroll to Sync Logs for the connected provider

sub_features.name: Webhooks
sub_features.description: Receive real-time events from POS providers (Square, Clover) when sales occur
sub_features.notes: Configured internally — no user action needed once connected

features.name: Automated Reorder Suggestions
features.description: AI-powered analysis of current stock and usage rates to tell you what to order and how much
how_to_use: Go to Reorder in the sidebar
Click Analyze Reorder Needs
System calculates based on current counts, usage rate, and par levels
Items appear in priority order: Urgent → Soon → Watch → OK
Toggle 'Show All' to also see items that are currently healthy

priority_levels.meaning: Days or less until out of stock — order immediately
Will run out this week — order soon
Not critical yet but worth monitoring
Stock is healthy — no action needed

features.name: Order Building & Sending
features.description: Build purchase orders from the suggestions and send them to vendors
how_to_use: On the Reorder page, items are pre-checked for Urgent and Soon
Adjust quantities in the qty fields if needed
Items are automatically grouped by vendor
For each vendor group:
Click 'Copy Order' to copy a formatted order to your clipboard
OR click 'Send Email' if the vendor has an email on file — sends automatically
OR copy/note items manually
To add items not in the system: type an item name + qty + unit in the 'Add extra item' field under a vendor group → click Add
features.order_email_format: TO: [Vendor] | RE: Purchase Order - [Your Bar Name] | DATE: [Today] | Itemized list of items + quantities

features.name: Vendor Management
features.description: Maintain a database of your distributors and suppliers with contact info
how_to_use: Go to Vendors in the sidebar
View all vendors in a list
Click New Vendor: enter name, email, phone, and optional fields
Edit: click a vendor row → update → save
Delete: click delete icon
Link a vendor to inventory items by setting 'Default Vendor' on each item

features.name: Historical Variance Reports
features.description: Browse all past variance calculation results with filtering and cost impact
how_to_use: Go to Variance Reports in the sidebar
Set optional filters:
Period Start / End dates
Status: All / Critical / Warning / Normal
Type: All Items / Beverages / Kitchen (Food)
Click Apply Filters
View summary KPIs: Est. Loss ($), Critical count, Warning count
View detailed table per item: expected vs actual, variance oz + %, cost impact, status badge

features.name: Profit Intelligence
features.description: Analyze which menu items are most (and least) profitable using sales data + recipe costs
how_to_use: Go to Profit Intelligence in the sidebar
Select a period preset (7d, 30d, this month) or set custom From/To dates
Click Run Analysis
View summary KPIs: Total Revenue, Total Cost, Total Profit, Avg Margin %
Filter by type: All / Drinks / Food
Sort table by: Profit, Margin %, Revenue, or Qty Sold
Click AI Insights for a natural-language breakdown of profitability trends
columns: Cost completeness indicator (full or partial recipe costs)

sub_features.name: AI Profit Insights
sub_features.description: Claude AI generates a markdown-formatted analysis of your profit data — top performers, margin opportunities, and trends
sub_features.how_to_use: Click AI Insights after running an analysis

sub_features.name: Category Split
sub_features.description: When both drinks and food are present, shows a breakdown section separating the two
sub_features.notes: Useful for restaurants with a bar — see if drinks or food drives more profit

sections.name: Administration (Internal Staff Only)
sections.notes: These features are only accessible to BarGuard internal admin accounts — not accessible to bar owners

features.name: Customer Account Management
features.description: View, edit, and manage all BarGuard customer accounts
how_to_use: Navigate to Admin panel (admin role required)
View all accounts with business name, plan, email, created date, partner affiliation
Search by name or email
Filter by partner
Click an account to expand:
Edit bar name or contact email
Change plan (manual override — any plan)
Send password reset email
Delete account (with confirmation)

features.name: Partner Account Management
features.description: Create and manage partner/vendor accounts that can view their linked bar accounts
how_to_use: Navigate to Admin → Partners
View all partners with name, code, status
Click a partner to see their linked bar accounts
Create new partner: enter name, partner code, contact info
Create partner login: generates login credentials for the partner
Delete partner if needed

sections.name: Partner Portal
sections.notes: For partners/vendors who manage multiple bar accounts — accessed via the partner login

features.name: Partner Dashboard
features.description: Overview of all bar accounts linked to the partner
how_to_use: Log in at /partner-login with partner credentials
View list of all assigned bars
See each bar's plan status and metrics

features.name: Homepage
features.description: Landing page with product overview, hero section, features, and CTA to sign up

features.name: How It Works
features.description: Step-by-step walkthrough of the BarGuard workflow with visuals

features.name: Pricing Page
features.description: Plan comparison and pricing — Basic $99, Pro $199, Enterprise $399 — with signup CTAs

features.name: FAQ
features.description: Common questions about getting started, features, integrations, and billing

features.name: Blog
features.description: Articles about bar management, loss prevention, and operations. Index at /blog, individual posts at /blog/[slug]

features.name: Partner Program Page
features.description: Information about the BarGuard partner/reseller program with a link to apply
how_to_use: Visit /partners
Read partner program details
Click Apply to submit an inquiry
Existing partners use the Partner Login link

features.name: Contact Page
features.description: Contact form for inquiries — submitted form is processed via /api/contact

features.name: Legal Pages
features.description: Privacy Policy (/privacy), Terms of Service (/terms), Refund Policy (/refund)

features.name: Status Page
features.description: Service uptime and status monitoring page at /status

features.name: Stripe Billing
features.description: Handles all subscription payments, upgrades, and billing management
capabilities: Checkout session creation for plan upgrades
Webhook handler syncing subscription status back to user account

features.name: Claude AI (Anthropic)
features.description: Powers all AI-driven features throughout the app
capabilities: Variance summary generation (natural language analysis of shift results)
Profit insights (trends and recommendations from profitability data)
Recipe generation (create ingredient lists from drink names)
Recipe matching (map uploaded recipes to inventory items)

features.name: Document OCR / Extraction
features.description: Extracts text and line items from uploaded invoice images and PDFs
capabilities: Parse item names and quantities from invoice photos

features.name: Email (Resend)
features.description: Transactional email delivery
capabilities: Reorder purchase order emails to vendors

features.name: Supabase (Database & Auth)
features.description: Postgres database with Row Level Security + authentication backend
capabilities: All data storage with per-business RLS isolation

features.name: Google Analytics / GTM
features.description: Usage and conversion tracking

sections.name: Core Data Model
sections.description: Key data structures powering the application
